reviews.review_text:
Amazing Stay!, perfect location, Mel at the reception was lovely!, the option to rent a firepit is great! Comes with wood and all!
The night was amazing, will come back surely
We had a great stay. The place is beautiful, everything was clean and well maintained, the fire pit was amazing, and Mel was incredibly helpful and friendly. Highly recommend!

Nice site, well run. Good mix of campers, tents and chalets.
Lovely hot showers clean toilet.
Quiet site.

This place is a little tired in some areas. It must have had lots of on-site cabins and these areas have been renovated when the cabins were removed. It is quiet little park a few km from town but all the people staying there seemed relaxed.

It has a fair few trees in the front area making parking some vans a bit tricky.

Out the back the sites were much more simple and trees provide a lovely outlook. It is on the budget end but relaxed and clean.

Een knus huisje, alles is aanwezig.
Locatie en omgeving waren perfect.
Zeker een aanrader! Mooie camping met ook huurhuisjes in alle soorten en maten.

Nice place.
Clean, quiet and well presented.
Close to the beach and shopping but far enough away not to be crazy busy...
Great staff.
Definitely be back 🇦🇺🤙

Great caravan park.  The staff couldn't be more friendly and helpful. Beautiful park, very clean facilities. A little out of town, worth the drive.  Specially with the King River Tavern almost next door.
Gorgeous shady park with grassed lots and good facilities. Loved sharing the park with all the doggos. All so well behaved.
Lovely little site on the edge of Busselton.
Good place to stay, nice relaxing environment. Pocket friendly.
In a great location, found it pretty quiet, it's an older caravan Park and some of the bays are a bit tight but all in all it's well located for looking around Busselton or looking around Dunsborough
Great place to stay, clean, tidy,  and the staff were really accomadating.
Close to town. Hot showers with super pressure. Great for hair wash days! 🤣 Facilities on the older side, but served us well for one night

The lady at reception (sorry I didn't get your name), went above and beyond to ensure we were comfortable during our stay. It is a peaceful park with ample peppermint trees and clean facilities. We even had a resident possum and her baby living in the trees above us.
Very enjoyable experience.

Stayed here a few times in the past and always enjoy how clean and well maintained the facilities are.
The only downside was the Wi-Fi which  wasn’t the most reliable and dropped out quite a lot. That said, it didn’t ruin what was otherwise a really great place to stay.

We stayed there for 2 nights. The whole place is old but is well maintained. Toilets and showers were very clean and hot showers was a plus. Kitchen facilities were good enough to be used for cooking and eating.
The place where we setup out camp had no tree shade which was bit of a let down. However, we have not stayed in the tent day time anyways so weren’t affected much.
Overall we had a pleasant stay which was both budget friendly and value for money.. staff was extremely helpful and accomodating.. we recommend them to other users
Stayed 9 nights on a powered caravan site it was awesome plenty of shade quite park

We booked 2 sites in June for ourselves and our son and family and were told Site 19 and 21 so we could be close together. When we arrived this week, we are on Site 71 and he is on Site 19. Very disappointing as the Receptionist said they can't promise anything. This is a yearly event for us to see him.

Nice budget caravan park,
Nice amenities, staff very helpful with any issues.
Had last minute availability and very reasonably priced.

Pet friendly

Its a little out of town, but it's a budget caravan park with clean bathrooms. The staff are brilliant, very friendly and so very helpful.
Its right on the highway so good if you're just needing to stop for a night or two. Stayed here with my campervan so no idea what the cabins are like. But the bathrooms are good - nice hot showers with GREAT shower pressure too!
Spacious camp sites with trees / shade. Clean and neat showers and toilets.
Lawn sites slightly aged facilities

Great park, friendly staff. Clean amenities, laundry. BBQs and camp kitchen. Lots of shady sites. Dump point. 10 minute drive to town. Turn at second entrance if coming from the north/town direction, caught us out.

Great location, easy check in and check out especially after hours. They kept us updated for our bookings and staff were really accommodating in putting us with our friends site. Amenities are clean, well kept just on the older side but everything worked great.

Lovely staff - were able to walk in last minute and had availability on the spot. (November is off-peak season, mind). Only thing to note is to have long extension cables for your powered sites, since our 15m wasn‘t long enough on site 37. 😆 they were friendly enough to lend us one that was much longer so thank you 🙏🏼 overall pleasant experience. Kitchen is a little small and more like a room than an outside kitchen, and hotplates were a little confusing to use but managed to navigate it all in the end. You meet some lovely people there and so many pooches 🐾
Happy to visit again ☺️

There is a bus service going in and out of busselton just outside the caravan park, and only a short walk from the beach🏝️
Goeie locatie. Prijs kwaliteit netjes!

BUSSELTON LAZY DAYS CARAVAN PARK
We went down to Busselton to check out the @caravanandcampingwa show and this is where we stayed.

📍 Location & Check-in
🚗 Distance: 229km South of Perth CBD
📚 Check-in: Quick easy process, all paid online. Run in grab the information sheet, gate fob and off you go.
🕐 In: 2pm for sites & cabins
⏰️ Out: 10am for both sites & cabins

🏕 Amenities Check
🛁 Toilets & Showers: Older style toilet block in the middle of the park. Very large with many clean toilets and showers.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family Room: No family room. There was a bench top sick in the female block.
🍳 Camp Kitchen: Situated in a demountable cabin turned camp kitchen. Very basic but has everything you need, plus couch and TV. Outdoor BBQ on a grassy patch with outdoor seating
🪁Kids Fun Factor: No kid factor but the grassed area near the toilet block to run and play

🍩 Coffee & Nearby Cafés
☕️ Onsite Coffee Van: No, Caravan coffee only
🧁 Closest Cafe/Food Spot: Cafes and take away close by (2 min drive)
🏭 Closest Township: Busselton 5 minute drive away. Has all major supermakes and everything you need.

🏠 Accommodation & Cost
⛺️ Cabins, powered and non powered sites available
💲We paid $130 for 3 night on a powered site
for: 2 Adults + 1 Child + 1 Infant & 2 Dogs
(Last minute deal)

🐾 Pet-Friendly
🐶 Dogs Allowed: Must be kept on leash at all times.

🗣 Heads Up!
🙋‍♀️ Wendy: shoutout to Wendy on the Desk, she was wonderful and shared best places to eat, activities and hints to find thr possums
🚦Busselton attractions: There is so many things to do. The foreshore is beautiful, so many play grounds and cafés, breweries, the jetty and the train. Just to name a few.
🔥 Fires: Permitted in season (have to hire fire pit and purchase wood from the office)
🏖 Beach: Gilly's Beach right next to @tonicbythebay and playground. Perfect spot.
🏃‍♂️Running: Mitch enjoyed running along the coastal track
🌳 Make sure you look up: The trees are filled with possums at night (nightly activity)

🚐 Would We Come Back?
Absolutely / Yes / Maybe / No
✅ YES! Great location, very relaxed and quite. Next time we wouldn't bring the dogs so we could do the jetty, train and underwater observatory. (No dogs on the jetty)

Follow along for more reviews and full video @aadriannaconnor
Financially Friendly, clean caravan park with older charm ||Was nice and quiete and perfect for resting after a busy day exploring
Walking distance to the beach. We enjoy it's lazy feel, and though it's not as flash as Mandalay we think it has a better ambience as its not as busy.
Great location. Bathrooms older, but very clean. Laundry needs more than 2 washing machines & 2 dryers. 6 of each would be much better.

Ideally situated, clean and well maintained. All linen, towels and kitchen utensils provided for use for all members of our family. Staff were friendly, respectful and helpful at all times. Looking forward to staying there again on a regular basis

Came very last minute as my partner and I went for a last minute road trip. Got to reception 10 minutes before closing and the lovely staff had no issue giving us a powered caravan spot for only 29$! Very satisfied, very clean toilet block, nice and quiet, overall great place.

The staff were friendly and helpful. The park has old, however, clean & tidy amenities.
The caravan sites can be a bit hit & miss in regards to how close you get to power & water. (We needed an extra long lead for power and 2 water hoses attached to reach the tap. The camp kitchen small and is fully enclosed, with air-conditioning, 2 induction cooktops, & T/V. The was no oven. 1 large group would fill the room. It was quiet when we used it so it was fine.
The park is situated about 7 minutes from town.
It just makes a 4 rating.

We stayed here for two nights and made a last-minute booking, so we didn’t expect much. However, we were thoroughly impressed by the comfort of the accommodation and the quality of the facilities. The unit was spotlessly clean, well maintained, smell good (or at least not smelly) and most importantly, had everything we needed for a comfortable stay — a new stove, clean and good-quality kitchen pots, pans, and utensils, efficient reversible air con. Tho it looks outdated from the outside, I think the staff are doing a great job at maintaining the unit. We enjoyed our stay very much. Big thank you!
Good quite park with large spots for big rigs. Enjoyed 3 days here visiting the sights.
Great experience for our holiday.
The staff were very courteous. The roads inside the park had a lot 9f pot holes which were filled with rain water
Horrible don’t stay here, there is no hot water in winter, the bed was tiny could fit me and my partner on it. Don’t waste your money there are better places for the same amount.

I'm impressed with the kitchen space.
Fridge and dish washing liquid and a space to eat if wanted.

There are clean toilets and showers with supplied toilet paper.

Close to the busselton Jetty. 1hr away from the Caves. Petrol station close to site and food markets.
Great for a solo traveller and those working, was down shooting photos for the triathalon for the weekend! Was able to charge my Tesla too which was nice!

Great welcome and service on check-in. Older style caravan park but very well kept.... plenty of trees for those hot summer months.  The ablution block is old but very clean toilets and plenty of hot water in the showers.

This is a nice park in a good location with friendly staff and some large grassy pitches. However, it is sadly let down by having only a single ablution block which is obviously not fit for purpose.

Midway through our stay the smell of sewage was so strong inside the block that it felt like it posed a health risk. It did fade again towards the end of our stay, but was always there. We asked at check out if they were having issues with it but were told, "no issues, but it can sometimes happen when there are lots of people". If it hadn't improved I was going to have to go and buy Vicks to apply below my nose before visiting. It was so bad you felt like it got in your clothes, I've never encountered anything like it.

The fact that management don't see this as an issue and therefore is unlikely to address it is a shame, because it will put us off staying again in the future.
Old park. Facilities need a makeover.

Highly recommended. We only stayed one night. We had forgotten our camp chairs and were kindly permitted to borrow 2 chairs from the camp kitchen. Thank you! The facilities are old but very well maintained. It had a lovely "this is what camping is all about feel to it". Reception was very friendly and lovely to deal with.

Nice simple, clean and well equipped accommodation at a reasonable price. Includes kitchen and equipment, microwave, fridge freezer, TV, hot water, 3 bedrooms.
Location close to a lovely quiet beach, but about 5 km from town center. Would stay there again.
Had a great site. Out of the way and easy to access.
Very dated facilities. Needs upgrading.

Really nice, shady park. Amenities are clean and well maintained. It's an older park, and doesn't have all the bells and whistles of some of the newer parks, but has everything you need and a good atmosphere. Dog friendly.
Stayed one night at a powered site. Called up last minute and they were very accommodating! Lovely clean park and great families, thank you!

From booking to arriving, the experience was excellent. Great caravan park . Well maintained, ammenities were fantastic, staff we dealt with were friendly, helpful and went out of their way to help. An added bonus is it’s dog friendly. Georgia on reception could not have been more helpful and lovely as was the young man who helped us with an issue we had with our caravan. The park has a camp kitchen, a very spacious ammenities and the showers were lovely and hot with great pressure. Great BBQ area which was cleaned everyday. I honestly have nothing negative to say about this park.
Fantastic caravan park, staff are friendly and helpful, ablutions are very clean, nice relaxing park.

This Summer Star park is a very dated caravan park, that is very expensive for what you get for a stay. The roads around the park site are not sealed and very dusty, grassed areas need a lot of work, the parks street lighting is inadequate.
They require a second ablution facility in the park site as it is a fair walk to get to from the outer campsites.
This weekend (08/03/2025) there was no power for the greater part of the day due to an electrical cabling upgrade and yet management of the park did mention this planned power outage when we booked. The booking staff should have informed patrons of this power outage and offered patrons a lower rate for the day. We would not have stayed here for the extra day if we had been forewarned of the power outage and were charged full rate, pretty cheeky of Lazy Days Caravan Park. We only were informed of the power outage 2 days previously to today.
Imagine if we had come here with no gas, there goes our fridge being kept cold. I know most people always have gas but there could be one that doesn’t have gas on board.
I would not recommend this park as a place to stay, there are better caravan parks around the area with more facilities, fairly priced, better roads, lighting and communication.
Very friendly staff and we had a big site.
One of the great old caravan parks of Busselton
Friendly staff, great facilities & location is only 10 minutes walk from the beach & 5 minutes drive into Busselton. Perfect stay for a weekend.
Just stayed in one of the 3 bed park homes - really clean, quiet, short walk to the beach, excellent value for money,  what more could anyone want?

We were left without water for 8 hours and when I went to reception to ask if some kind of reimbursement could be arranged I was told a very smug “no I won’t be doing THAT”
I emailed Gday parks head office directly as reception refused to give me any contact details for management, they advised they would forward the communication to the owners, who are yet to reply. We were advised via txt to disconnect from mains until the work was complete, never received another txt advising we could now reconnect however reception was helpful enough to say “the waters been on and off since 10am, it’s on and off” and “just use the park washing machines then” (at $5 a load for what looked like about a 4/5kg washing machine I would have had to spend an additional $25/$30 on top of what I already paid for the site yet I couldn’t have a shower/flush our toilet or drink water that had run through our filtration system.

We just returned home after spending 3 nights in one of the cabins here . Absolute great experience, cozy cabin , clean and comfy . Everyone was great . Over my stay I spoke to two ladies on the reception area and they were so accomadating and friendly . Highly recommend!!!
A very nice park. My site was a bit tight. Very pleasant help staff.

Would not recommend the chalets during summer here.

We booked over Xmas and the 2 bedroom chalet we had been assigned was so old and dodgy compared to the photos shown online. It states online that the rooms have slight variations but this was gross and not even close to what was being advertised.

We originally booked for 6 nights, but after 1 night we had to leave. The air con was struggling to get the chalet cool and we had 3 power outages as everything in the unit kept tripping. With two young children - we had to leave as it was as if we were stuck in a sweat box.

In saying all of this, the service of the maintenance guy and the lovely girl behind the counter was great. It’s just ashame that the accommodation was so bad and in desperate need of an upgrade as well as being totally falsely advertised!!!!

We were told upon leaving that we would receive a partial refund for the difference due to the above issues. This was back on the 21st of December. We were told it would take time due to public holidays etc to come through. We are now going on 3 weeks and still nothing!!! I called today and talked to another lovely lady, but unfortunately she had no idea what I was talking about as there were no notes on the system?!?!?I’m annoyed to say the least.

It was great
Reduced a star because the WiFi didn't work.
Very professional and helpful staff, Mel is great! We stayed there two nights in a powered site. The park is well maintained and is good value for money. We would definitely stay there again!

Terrible Experience – Mismanagement and Rudeness

We had a horrible experience at this campsite. Months ago, we called to reserve a night, but when we arrived, they claimed we had booked the wrong date. They also insisted they had called to inform us, but we never received any calls and were never out of signal range.

The receptionist was extremely rude to my friend, who had made the booking. At the time of the reservation, they said they would send a confirmation email, but it was never sent. Now, we’re left without a place to stay for the night because of their mistakes and false claims.

To make matters worse, we haven’t even been refunded for the money we paid. I would not recommend this place to anyone. The lack of professionalism and customer service is unacceptable.

Great caravan park - staff got me the closest spot to the toilets for my elderly mother . Pay by card for the night 🌙 fits with their company policy & everything is smooth sailing . Well worth the stay . Well maintained 👍 park

Had a last minute desire to spend a week in Busselton Xmas Holidays early Jan, knew most parks would be full with no availability but thought we would try anyway. Rang Lazy Days Summerstar and spoke with Mel, she was very professional and went over and above with helpful service to accommodate us. Thanks for the friendly and very helpful service Mel, looking forward to our stay at Summerstar Lazy Day's Tourist Park
Cheers Wayne
Very comfortable. Stayed at the chalet.. awesome location. Relaxing atmosphere. Nice trees. Plenty shade to relax. A must in Busselton.
Great place rooms are great for a weekend or whatever you need close to town very quiet place 😀
Plenty of options, camp, RV or chalets.
Spacious site, good shade, very well maintained amenities, pleasant feel about the site, good staff. Thanks!

Nice spot. Beautiful trees and plenty of shade.
Nice and quiet, however it is mid week, that may be a different story on weekends. Sites are a little close together but thats minor really providing you have respectful neighbours.

環境安靜，四人房一晚152$，屋內設施一應俱全非常滿意！廚房鍋碗瓢盆、餐廳、客廳再加上小露台，可惜只待一晚~
周遭的景點餐廳也都蠻豐富，原本只是隨意找個過夜的地方，但意外的滿意👍👍👍 即便設施有點老舊，可是非常的乾淨舒適~所有的工作人員也都很親切喔👍🇹🇼

Stayed here three nights mid week Oct 2024 being out of peak time.  This caravan park is 6km out of town west from the main CBD.  Ideal for us as we liked to ride our bikes to and from town and prefer the more laid back vibe that parks out of town provide.  This park has fencing on all four sides with access to the park restricted via access card.  A big reason we choose Lazy Days is due to fencing and security in place.  The three C/parks located on Maritime Pde in town have no fencing at all on any side allowing unfettered public access throughout the parks with lack of security accompanying contemporary reports of thefts from camp sites.  Lazy Days is a little older but none the less delivers all you need for caravanning as an adult couple without kids not needing the bells and whistles and accompanying prices.  Our site was huge with lots of space around us.   Toilets are functional and cleaned daily with good hot and cold water pressure.  Pet friendly.  Booking in was easy with friendly, polite and engaging front counter staff.  This park has a mix of onsite accomodation and what appears to be a few permanent residents.  Beach about a ten minute walk.  Park lacks asphalt roads and gutters however not an issue for us.  Happy to recommend and if budget conscious this will suit you fine.  Stay well and travel safe.
A unique living with nature experience. Not your ordinary brick and mortar type hotel. A rustic style living but with decent amenities. Highly Recommended.👍😀
It was an older caravan park. it was ok very dark at night and you had to have pin to come and go
Simple old school caravan park. Big sites and amenities are clean.

It's definitely a caravan park for couples.
No kids  play equipment, which means no screaming children all day.
Very friendly and helpful staff.
Great clean caravan park. The special deal they have on atm for Winter is a great price 👍.

Nice n quiet tidy and affordable

$25 pernight

Midway through a 4 week stay.
Great spacious sites, ours has a side cement slab so no sand or dirt carried in to van.  Laundry is good,   amenities whilst older style are good and clean.  Water pressure is good.
Great winter rates and enjoying our stay.  Staff very helpful when needed and park is tidy and relaxing.  Access to several dog friendly beaches within 5 mins drive, shopping places the same and this week we discovered Tonic by the Bay restaurant/Cafe right by the beach....perfect.
The sites are a bit higly pigly. There were rats in the otherwise clean toilet block. Staff friendly. Short driving distance to town centre.

We absolutely loved this place. Georgia and her partner were A1 and pro. We extended for one day than another all up 4 days. Would of stayed longer but had to move on. Amenities are old but impeccable. Same as the laundry. Very happy time here. Thanks guys😍
We are staying with our caravan
Lovely and quiet caravan park. Ablution block is very clean. Easy drive into Bunbury and Margaret River region.
Nice quiet, roomy, shady, level caravan park.

Good location, staff were friendly, large sites and dark at night.

Ablutions block was recently painted, but it just covered all of the issues - broken tiles, broken wood, leaking taps.  The camp kitchen was awful - dirty, no hot water, and hadn't been cleaned properly in a while.  Wouldn't consider it a real camp kitchen - at most a very basic kitchenette.
Absolutely enjoyed our stay the cabin was clean and tidy,the staff were friendly and so helpful The park is very clean
Had a long weekend here, definitely worth that money we paid for

Loved this campground. Nice large sites, large clean amenities (shower set up is a bit odd) but it works and the water is super hot and great pressure.

Small laundry area, the machines work well. Washer is card operated and dryer is coin.

Nice location, easy to walk to town along the beachfront path.

we really liked the caravan park and the lady at the reception was lovely and very helpful! however, one of the care takers was responsible for our very uncomfartable stay here. our puppy was greeting people who walked by, when he came up and started yelling at me for the barking. just to be clear, our puppy doesn’t bark at night or for long times, just when he’s greeting people! after that we didn’t feel welcome at our site anymore and tried to be away for the most part of the day.
Nice place beach close

4th stay at this caravan park.
Ablutions very clean, but a complete dust bowl in the camping sites.
Love the location but price has been increased by too much, almost double the price from 2 years ago!

Cheapest option around for accommodation while not feeling "too" cheap. Quiet area, easy to get some rest on a long journey.

Don't forget to visit Sharp's Vintage Motor Museum just 10mins drive away.
Bathroom is dry and clean, light is bright, place is quiet, garbage area is also clean. Eight minutes drive from jetty. We will come back again for sure 😀
Very old tried park. While there power outage every day and water pressure was not good. Glad to be out of there.

We enjoyed one night at this caravan park. Gravel/dirt roads. Our site had a concrete slab plus dirt. Shaded by trees. Amenities are very clean, and they look freshly painted. Reception lady very friendly and accommodating, as we booked about an hour before we arrived. Park is full. Great TV reception and Telsta reception. Would definitely stay again.
It was great value and I had a nice spot under a tree. They have unpowered sites and are pet friendly.
Lovely place to stay. Walking distance to beach, quiet location. Amenities are excellent

Great quiet spot for $30 for unpowered site to clean the dust off after a week out bush
Showers were so hot and heaps of power
Great spot, but the price is a bit high when public holidays. I stayed 1 night on 28/1 it was $86 a night, we were surprised. The toilets and showers were nice and clean, strong water pressure.
Great stay again but get annoyed when people waste water washing their caravans and cars
Lovely caravan park, we had a shady spot for our caravan, clean amenities.

Good value especially over a long weekend.
But really time for an update.
Everything was clean but looks straight out of the 70's

Wir haben dort 1 Nacht mit unserem Campervan verbracht, obwohl wir eigentlich 2 Nächte gebucht hatten, wir mussten allerdings die zweite Nacht stornieren, weil wir wegen eines Notfalls zurück nach Deutschland mussten, die zweite Nacht wurde ohne Probleme storniert und das Geld zurück überwiesen, ein guter und ruhiger Caravan-Park, sehr nettes Personal, nicht weit vom Strand entfernt (fußläufig erreichbar), WiFi vorhanden, insgesamt sehr empfehlenswert****
Lovely place to stay will do it again they make you very welcoming

The studio cabin is absolutely disappointing & not worth $99.00. We understand the basic nature of this studio (no ensuite/toilet or Air Conditioning) but we were not informed that the studio cabin was opposite the ablution block & dump point.|To add to that there are two big extremely bright fluorescent lights shining off the ablution block wall right into our accommodation. These will be on all night. The curtain is "paper thin" & doesn't even cover the whole door.|The inconvenience of a "well lit" cabin all night is extremely frustrating.|Will definitely not recommend this studio cabin.|Do better Lazy Days Caravan Park, at least update the curtains.|Have taken a photo of the lights but can't post it.|There are ants all over the kitchen area.
Clean toilets and showers. Pleasant reception and nice staff!
We used our caravan. Facilities on camp were very good, clean, plenty of pressure and hot water.
Great place wish we stayed longer and awesome breakfast

We only booked 2 nights as a weekend getaway but when arriving at the park was shocked to see the rundown and small bay for our Motorhome.  The slab was severely cracked with very little grass on our door entry.  Yes, the park is clean but it needs a renovation to bring it up to date.  Good location.  We left after 1 day.

Was a good stay we were there for 2 weeks the other campers were very friendly and the bird life was amazing along with the ring tail possums were a funny sight all in all a good place to be lazy . 😀  perfect name for a great place , will be staying there again
Great place
Plenty of old school charm about this park. Heaps of shady trees. Real cheery welcome from the lady at reception. Great name for a caravan park.

3 Stars..Are for the cleaner Guy very Nice n reception lady👍 never met a cleaner that will let u in whilst he's cleaning that was Lovely of him ...Taken 1Star away on account of the old guy that rides around on his Motored Bike...Complaining about an awning that had the walls up on a site we paid for and other things...Other than that was a Good stay...
Great caravan park, spotless amenities and lovely bush scenery and sounds.

my family and i stayed at lazy days caravan park in one of there shacks, was very enjoyable. old style but clean and updated!! the lady at the reception desk was so lovely we will definitely be coming back soon!

This is an older park that I am told used to have lots of permanents. However, owners are slowly but surely transforming it back to a tourist park.

This will take time, but the managers are doing a great job and are very friendly and obliging.

Amenities block whilst old is very clean and looks to have recently been painted.

The park is well located and a good choice to base yourself and explore the area.

We booked for a week and felt so comfortable here we have extended for an extra week.
Pretty tired compared to some of the parks we stayed in

This caravan park is excellent, facilities are clean and well maintained. Campers kitchen nice. It is a small park compared to the others but it is the best, sites good size, close to everything, office staff lovely.  Has all you need to discover Busselton and Margaret River.
We stayed in the caravan park and it was fantastic. They helped us to our site, large sites, and very clean.
Had relxing experience at the park, staff at reception were great. Cottages/cabins were old but clean, with utensils provided. Very relaxed environment, simply had few lazy days with family.

Parked up on the most green grassy unpowered spot I'd seen in many months of full time travel! Was a perfect base for us to explore for a few days. Book swap in reception well timed to update my collection of reads. Toilets/bathrooms large and always very clean. Laundry excellent as it used tap and pay! Thank you for a wonderful time
We loved our stay. It's perfect and very reasonable price.

Loved this place!!! Great value, friendly and a lovely low key caravan park for adults with no kids. The amenities are old but freshly painted and very clean. There are lovely shady trees everywhere and tidy grounds. Only a block away from the bike path that runs all the way along the foreshore to the jetty.

Great little place. Stayed at a cabin with 2 rooms with the family. Kids loved it. Had all necessary household items which I was impressed. Even had WIFI which was a pleasant surprise. We would definitely come through again.

we had a powered site; we were in a camper trailer in I'm sure the furthest corner from the ablution block. we were three adults in this bay, admin incorrectly booked us in as two adults, but we payed for three adults.|manager Dave came along and accused my husband not so politely as to only payed for two. well, he had egg on his face when i went to the office and admin lady said yes, theyve paid for three.|even if he was right his attitude was terrible. won't stay there again.|

Great park while facilities are not the most modern they were spotless, nice trees surround the park. Reasonable access for your van with plenty of space between van sites||One nice thing you were guided into your caravan site by one of the staff
Lovely little camping spot, very peaceful clean and well maintained. Receptionist was lovely too. Would recommend to anyone✨

Perfect spot . I have been coming here for years but this time got the SHOCK OF MY LIFE…. Winter period/ No school holidays/ caravan park as good as empty ….PRICE $49.20 Per night after $3 pensioner discount !!!!!!!!! I call this legal theft so…. NEVER AGAIN AT THIS RIDICULOUS PRICE !
Only visiting

We stayed here for 2 nights in a powered campsite the caravan park is very run down it needs some money spent on it, the ablutions are dirty and management decided to put us in a camping bay next to a noisy couple who seemed like they were living there permenently  in their tent/homeless shelter two run down vehicles and nuisance barking dog, there was plenty of other areas for management to allocate us a bay so very average effort by management, wont be staying here again no way

Found this little gem from other Google reviews. It's off season so caravan site with power is $25 per night. Showers are dated but immaculately clean. Wendy in reception is wonderful, very eager to help and suggest places to visit. Shower block being painted so currently unisex but Wendy explained that when we checked in. Nicely spread out and not jammed in like sardines like some caravan parks. They had a lot of rain recently and are in the process of fixing the potholes that another reviewer over exaggerated about. Originally were only staying 1 night but are onto our 3rd night and about to extend.

Very old and run down. The roads were badly pot holed to the point I thought I’d damage my car & van. Badly in need of some TLC and maintenance amenities Bradley need looking at, rotted wooden bench, shower heads face the wrong way. The mens toilet roof leaks badly when it rains right over the cubicles.
The manager I spoke to (Wendy) was very nice and helpful.
Great location - good deal during winter $25 per night - Dated amenities but the cleanest site we’ve been too 👌 short walk to the beach also
Excellent location close to the beach which was good for  exercising our dog. Friendliest staff, excellent facilities, immaculately clean.  Highly recommend 👌
Great clean and very handy location. Managers were extremely helpful and friendly people. It's an older park and they are striving to carry out improvements. Well done.
Beautiful peaceful park and Wendy an Dave owners are the perfect host's

We stayed at a family cabin here for a single night. It was very comfortable and spacious. The owners could improve the cleanliness a little by vacuuming the cobwebs from the corners of the ceilings and the sliding doors. The heater in the cabin had next to no heat coming out if it so thankfully it wasn’t too cold. I don’t know if they need serviced or the heating element is kaput. Other than that I had no complaints - the hot water was very hot and quick to come through. The park is in a good spot too with amenities close by.

Stayed for two nights. Took advantage of special rate offer. Great little park not far out of the city. Great service from staff. Nice and quiet, good sites and facilities are fine. Washing machines are card operated which is good. Dryers take coins. Recommend.
